On Small E-Readers and Server-Side Rendering

By A. Writer, 2026-07-10

Constrained devices force clarity. When a reader has a few hundred kilobytes of RAM, the only sane place to do the heavy lifting is on a server that has none of those constraints. This is not a limitation so much as a healthy separation of concerns between production and consumption.

Why one document per feed

A flat folder browser makes dozens of tiny files unusable. Grouping a run's articles into a single document, one chapter each, matches how the device actually presents a library to the reader.

  • Each article becomes a chapter.
  • Images are stripped; the screen is monochrome.
  • Styling is dropped so the reader's typography wins.
The device is a dumb consumer, and that is the entire point.

Extraction is the hard part: paywalls, JavaScript-only pages, and boilerplate all conspire against a clean body. A good extractor earns its keep here.
